User Story: As a user, I want to enroll in an edition so that I gain access to the course
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): Edition must be open (not past/full/closed). Capacity checked with row-level lock. Status Confirmed (no approval & no tasks) or Pending (approval OR tasks). LMS access granted immediately only for Confirmed. Errors: edition_past, edition_full, enrollment_closed, already_enrolled.

User Story: As a trainer, I want to enroll a colleague by email so that I can register them without an existing account
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): Email lookup; if absent, user created with auto username+password; enroller stored in enrolled_by; wp_new_user_notification sent; existing colleague profile fields NOT overwritten.

User Story: As a trainer, I want to enroll an existing colleague so that no duplicate account is created
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): User resolved by email; enrollment_data persisted per-registration but user meta NOT mutated (anti account-hijack).

User Story: As a user, I want my enrollment form to update my profile so that details are remembered next time
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): Name/phone/organisation/department/billing/VAT/invoice email/GLN/company stored to user_meta via getUserMetaMapping(). Colleague enrollments skip this.

User Story: As a user, I want to fill personal details so that the provider has my contact info
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): Fields first/last/email/phone/organisation/department. Required validated. Stored enrollment_data['enrollment_personal'] with submitted_at/by envelope.

User Story: As a user, I want to enter billing details so that invoicing is accurate
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): company/vat/address/postcode/city/gln/invoice_email validated required per stage. Stored enrollment_data['enrollment_billing'] + reserved keys to user_meta.

User Story: As a user, I want to select sessions I'll attend so that the provider knows my preferences
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): Session IDs validated vs availability (selection_open, deadline). Stored registration.selections JSON. Initial recorded in enrollment_data.initial_selection.phases[] (append-only).

User Story: As a user, I want to accept terms before enrolling so that I acknowledge obligations
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): terms_accepted boolean required. If false: 'Je moet akkoord gaan met de voorwaarden.'

User Story: As a user, I want to enroll in an approval-required edition so that the trainer can review
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): Status Pending; LMS access NOT granted; message 'Je inschrijving is ontvangen en wacht op goedkeuring.'

User Story: As a user/system, I want a quote auto-generated after enrollment so that billing is tracked
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): Quote on registration/created (skip Interest/Waitlist; defer Pending+tasks). Voucher discount applied. Linked via registration.quote_id.

User Story: As a user, I want to apply a voucher code so that I get a discount
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): Validated via VoucherService::validateVoucher(). Valid → € X,XX. Failure: 'Vouchercode ongeldig of verlopen.'

User Story: As a system, I want to reject enrollment in a past edition so that users don't enroll in past offerings
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): isPast() checked regardless of admin status. Error 'Deze editie is voorbij'.

User Story: As a system, I want to prevent over-booking so that capacity is respected
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): START TRANSACTION + FOR UPDATE lock; confirmed>=capacity → ROLLBACK + 'This edition is full'.

User Story: As a system, I want to prevent duplicate enrollments so that a user can't register twice
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): findByUserAndEdition() in transaction. Interest/Waitlist/Cancelled reactivatable; others block 'User is already enrolled'.

User Story: As a system, I want to upgrade anonymous interest to confirmed enrollment so that prior intent is preserved
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): Self-enrol only. Match email + Interest + no user_id. Sets user_id, updates status, merges enrollment_data (existing precedence).

User Story: As a user, I want to re-enroll after cancelling so that I can try again
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): Cancelled/Interest/Waitlist reactivatable. enrollment_data preserved unless new provided (merged).

User Story: As a user, I want a simpler 2-step form for online courses so that I sign up faster
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): isOnline() (online/webinar/e-learning) → skips billing step; personal + confirm only.

User Story: As a system, I want immediate enrollment without a form so that open/free courses enroll instantly
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): enrollment_form='direct' → auto-enrolls + redirects. Form template refuses to render.
Errors Found: Test failed: no registration created on direct-enroll visit. Root cause: stale re-seed left 3 duplicate 'direct' editions; test grabbed a past-dated one (status meta=open but isPast()) so enroll() correctly returned edition_past. NOT a product bug — direct-enroll path + isPast guard both correct.
P3 Fix Status: FIXED: clean unseed+reseed (1 future-dated direct edition); hardened findEditionByCourseTitlePrefix to require open AND non-past (INV-7 effective-status alignment)
P4 Re-test: PASS — OnlineEnrollmentCest 9/9 green after seed clean + selector hardening

User Story: As a system, I want a closed state when enrollment isn't possible so that users understand why
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): enrollment_mode='closed' when status disallows + insufficient spots. Error state rendered.

User Story: As a system, I want to auto-populate company_id so that company billing is captured
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): CompanyAffiliation::getCompanyId() stored on registration row. Colleague path also stores.

User Story: As an anonymous visitor, I want to register interest so that I'm notified when it opens
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): Anonymous Interest (user_id NULL). No capacity check/LMS access. Edition must allow interest (Announcement OR klassikaal-no-sessions). One row per email per edition.

User Story: As a user, I want to register interest in an upcoming offering so that I'm contacted
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): Checks user's active registrations; error if already interested/enrolled/waitlisted.

User Story: As a visitor, I want an interest questionnaire so that my answers are recorded
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): Appends enrollment_data['interest']. Re-submission merges by email.

User Story: As a user, I want to join the waitlist when full so that I'm contacted if a spot opens
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): Only when status=Full; else 'waitlist_closed'. No capacity check. Duplicate check on active reg.

User Story: As a visitor, I want to answer waitlist questions so that the provider learns about me
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): Appends enrollment_data['waitlist']. Re-submission merges by email+edition.

User Story: As a system, I want to track required completion steps so that I know what a user must do
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): buildInitialTasks() from edition meta (session_selection/questionnaire/documents/approval). Each pending, phase='enrollment'. Stored JSON completion_tasks.

User Story: As a user, I want to select session times so that I attend at a suitable time
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): type='session_selection'. Waits for approval if prior approval task. Opens if selection_open + before deadline. Re-editable if not started + deadline not passed.

User Story: As a user, I want to answer intake questions before start so that the provider understands my background
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): type='questionnaire'. Available immediately. Validated per stage. Status stays Pending. Stored enrollment_data['intake'].

User Story: As a user, I want to upload proof documents so that my qualifications are recorded
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): type='documents'. Max 5 files, 10MB, PDF/JPEG/PNG only (.docx excluded). Stored in protected dir. Attachment linked via post meta.

User Story: As a trainer, I want to review/approve documents so that I verify readiness
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): type='approval'. Available after questionnaire+documents complete. Pending until admin confirms. Unlocks post-course tasks.

User Story: As a system, I want to block session selection until approval so that confirmed attendees are tracked
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): session_selection locked until approval complete. Reason 'Wacht op goedkeuring.'

User Story: As a system, I want approval locked until tasks done so that only complete submissions reviewed
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): approval locked until questionnaire+documents complete. Reason 'Wacht op vragenlijst en documenten.'

User Story: As a system, I want to auto-confirm pending registration after enrollment tasks done so that user gains access
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): onTaskCompleted(); if no post-course tasks → confirmRegistration() → Confirmed + LMS access. Logged.

User Story: As a user, I want to give feedback after finishing so that the provider improves
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): type='post_evaluation' phase='post_course'. Available post-completion. Stored enrollment_data['evaluation'].

User Story: As a user, I want to upload completion proof so that I document my achievement
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): type='post_documents'. Same upload restrictions. Appends to post-course task data.

User Story: As a trainer, I want to sign off post-course documents so that I verify completion
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): type='post_approval'. Locked until post_evaluation+post_documents. When all done → status Completed.

User Story: As a system, I want to avoid empty task lists so that only relevant tasks show
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): buildInitialTasks() returns [] if none enabled; initialize skips empty.

User Story: As a user, I want to change session preferences until start so that I update availability
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): Re-available if completed + selection_open + not started + deadline not passed.

User Story: As a system, I want to lock session selection after deadline so that the schedule is final
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): Locked if selection_deadline<now. Reason 'De deadline voor sessiekeuze is verstreken.'

User Story: As a user/admin, I want to download proof documents so that I can review/archive
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): handleDownloadProof() resolves attachment. Authz owner OR stride_manage. Must be in protected dir. Stored MIME.

User Story: As a system, I want ID-bearing docs in a deny-ruled dir so that they're not web-accessible
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): ensureProtectedDir() .htaccess deny. Path validated against protected base.

User Story: As a system, I want only owner/admin to download proofs so that PII isn't leaked
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): resolveProofDownload(): user_id===userId OR stride_manage. Identical error not-found/not-owned (no oracle).

User Story: As a system, I want required fields so that critical info is captured
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): 'required' flag per field. Empty → '%s is verplicht.' Errors aggregated into WP_Error.

User Story: As a system, I want scale fields validated so that responses stay in range
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): type='scale' int between min(1)/max(5). '%s moet tussen X en Y liggen.'

User Story: As a system, I want to warn on reserved field names so that admins don't override profile data
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): getUserMetaMapping() reserved keys; settings page warns on match but still allows save.

User Story: As a user, I want to answer intake questions so that the trainer knows my starting level
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): Stage 'intake'. Filled after enrollment confirmed/pending.

User Story: As a user, I want a course evaluation so that my feedback improves future offerings
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): Stage 'evaluation'. Only after status=Completed (validated).

User Story: As a user, I want to enroll in a trajectory and auto-receive all mandatory courses so that I don't enroll in each separately
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): enroll(): checks open+capacity. Parent reg (trajectory_id set, edition_id NULL). cascadeOnEnrollment(): edition→child reg; pure-LD→grant LD + _stride_trajectory_courses. WP_Error enrollment_closed/no_capacity.

User Story: As a cohort user, I want cancelled children to cascade when the parent is cancelled so that access is consistent
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): onRegistrationCancelled() isTrajectoryParent(). cascadeOnCancellation() (cohort only). cancelChildren(), revoke LD per child + pure-LD grants.

User Story: As a user, I want to select electives and have only chosen editions created so that I only pay for what I chose
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): setSelections/setSelectionsFromCourses: active regs only, window open, min_choices per group. Saves JSON. WP_Error incomplete_choices/too_many_choices/edition_full.

User Story: As the system, I want selections to never break required courses so that mandatory editions can't be de-selected
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): cascadeOnSelection() reads mandatoryEditionIds(); skips cancelling mandatory children. Partial success on capacity errors.

User Story: As the system, I want re-selecting a cancelled elective to reset its status so that state stays consistent
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): createChildRegistration(): cancelled child same parent → reactivate (status to parent, clear cancelled_at). Grants LD if Confirmed.

User Story: As the system, I want child creation capacity checked atomically so that parallel selections can't both take the last spot
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): reserveEditionCapacityAndCreateChild(): transaction + countConfirmedForUpdate() FOR UPDATE. full → ROLLBACK + edition_full.

User Story: As the system, I want a free trajectory with paid children to auto-generate child quotes so that billing is correct
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): maybeCreateChildQuote(): trajectory price>0→no child quote; else edition price>0→createQuote() with parent billing/user-meta fallback. Fail→log, never block.

User Story: As the system, I want cohort status changes to propagate to children so that all advance together
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): onRegistrationConfirmed()→cascadeOnStatusChange(). Skips terminal children. Grant/revoke LD if access changed. Self-paced no-op.

User Story: As the system, I want pure-LD courses granted/revoked idempotently so that access matches registration
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): grantPureLdAccess(): dup check then grant+append meta. revokePureLdGrantsForParent(): revoke per course + remove entries.

User Story: As a cohort user with electives, I want the deadline to lock selections so that I can't change after the window
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): areSelectionsLocked() = deadline OR manual lock. setSelectionsFromCourses() → WP_Error('choices_locked').

User Story: As the system, I want selection phases recorded so that we can audit choice history
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): applySelections()→appendInitialSelectionPhase() append-only {phase,edition_ids,course_ids}. Latest = source of truth.

User Story: As a user, I want group min_choices enforced so that I can't select too few/too many from one group
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): validateSelections(): required||1. count intersect. < incomplete_choices; > too_many_choices. No children on fail.

User Story: As a user, I want a trajectory dashboard showing progress so that I know what's left
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): getProgressData(): per course isComplete()/enrolled. Aggregates completed/in_progress vs total_required. Per-course state badges.

User Story: As an admin, I want backfill-cascade CLI to re-materialize missing children so that historical data is complete
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): wp stride trajectory backfill-cascade --commit: parents→cascadeOnEnrollment (skip existing)+cascadeOnSelection (reactivate). Dry-run default. Idempotent.

User Story: As a visitor, I want a dateless klassikaal edition to show 'Toon interesse' so that I know I can only express interest
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): getEffectiveStatus→'announcement'. card is_interest → 'Geen datum — toon interesse', CTA 'Toon interesse'. Online dateless stay 'open'.

User Story: As a visitor, I want 'Nog X plaatsen'→'Volzet' as capacity fills so that I know if I can enroll
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): spots 1-5 → variant 'few' 'Nog X plaats(en)'. spots=0→updateStatus(Full). spot opens on Full→back to Open. Cache invalidated.

User Story: As a visitor, I want a cancelled edition shown muted with 'Bekijk alternatieven' so that I understand it won't happen
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): is_cancelled→opacity-85, muted title, 'Deze editie werd geannuleerd', price hidden, CTA 'Bekijk alternatieven'.

User Story: As a visitor, I want past editions to auto-show 'Completed' and be filtered so that I don't enroll in something that happened
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): query filters status IN activeValues() AND date window. getEffectiveStatus: isPastDates & not terminal→Completed. Terminal wins.

User Story: As a visitor, I want klassikaal editions with zero sessions to show 'Announcement' so that I can only express interest
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): isClassroom && publishedSessionCount==0 → Announcement (overrides Open). Online no-session stays stored.

User Story: As an enrolled visitor, I want '✓ Ingeschreven'+progress or '✓ Afgerond' so that I know my status
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): progress>=100→'✓ Afgerond'; >0→'X% voltooid'; else '✓ Ingeschreven'. Not when cancelled.

User Story: As a visitor, I want free editions to show 'Gratis' so that I see no cost
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): is_free = price<=0 → 'Gratis' badge + footer label.

User Story: As a visitor, I want a 'Lopend' badge on a running edition so that I know classes are happening
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): OfferingStatus::InProgress→'Lopend' variant 'online'. Admin sets status when sessions start.

User Story: As a visitor on /klassikaal, I want editions grouped into 3 date bands with dateless on page 1 so that I find upcoming+coming-soon
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): order_into_bands: A dated>=today ASC, B dateless, C dated<today ASC. Page-1 guard reserves countB slots. Excludes online-only.
P2 Test Result: PASS (acceptance CatalogShakeoutCest bands; + card-count test fixed)
Errors Found: CatalogShakeoutCest::klassikaalRendersCardsForAnonymous asserted querySelectorAll('article') >=1, but catalog cards are <a> roots (card-edition.php/card-course.php) — selector matched 0 though /klassikaal renders 25 cards. Stale-test/green-blind-spot, not a product bug.
P3 Fix Status: FIXED: count ':scope > a' card anchors instead of 'article' (3 selectors + uniqueness selector)

User Story: As a visitor on /online, I want a flat grid of online editions + pure-LD courses so that I browse always-on options
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): online_items(): active online editions (incl dateless) + pure-LD online courses with zero editions. No band ordering.

User Story: As a visitor, I want catalog cards batch-prefetched so that browsing 24+ cards has no N+1
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): render_cards()+prefetch: batched getEffectiveStatuses+countByEditions+enrolled-set+LD progress. Card partials pure renderers.
P2 Test Result: PASS (integration; CatalogEndpointTest guest-wire fixed for seed isolation)
Errors Found: CatalogEndpointTest::guestCanFetchNonceAndPageThroughRealHttp asserted its own edition lands on page 1 — seed-fragile (band ordering + 28 seed editions paged it off).
P3 Fix Status: FIXED: assert rendered-card shape (/edities/ anchor) not specific permalink

User Story: As a visitor, I want pure-LD course cards to show best edition + my completion state so that I know if enrolled
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): prefetch_course_cards(): ranks enrollable(2)>active(1). user_state completed>in_progress>enrolled. CTA per state.

User Story: As a visitor, I want theme tabs on /klassikaal and /online so that I see only relevant courses
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): theme tabs from stride_theme. setFilter()→stride_catalog_page. Counts pre-computed. Rolls back on fetch failure.

User Story: As a visitor, I want 'Toon meer' to server-render the next slice so that the grid expands without reload
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): loadMore()→stride_catalog_page returns {html,count}. insertAdjacentHTML beforeend. hasMore from shown vs total.
P2 Test Result: PASS (acceptance CatalogShakeoutCest Toon meer — skipped when below cap)

User Story: As a visitor on a course page, I want the best active edition in the sidebar so that I know what's enrollable
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): single-sfwd-courses.php sidebar via primary_edition. No edition→generic online badge + pure-LD flow.

User Story: As the system, I want a warning if the 500-item cap is exceeded so that I know the list is truncated
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): warn_if_capped(): count>=500 logs 'catalog enumeration filled cap'.

User Story: As the system, I want editions of unpublished courses excluded so that draft/trashed courses don't leak
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): edition_items_from_ids(): get_post_status!='publish'→skip. Defensive at card-edition.php.

User Story: As a trainer, I want to mark a user present/absent/excused atomically so that data is reliable
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): mark(): resolves editionId. record(): existing→UPDATE (idempotent), else INSERT. Fires stride/attendance/marked. WP_Error on DB fail.

User Story: As a trainer, I want to re-mark without duplicates so that correcting is safe
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): record(): findBySessionAndUser→UPDATE or INSERT. No duplicates.

User Story: As the system, I want Present to count and Absent/Excused not to so that reports reflect attendance
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): AttendanceStatus: Present countsAsAttended()=true; Absent/Excused=false. Hours/rate/presentUserIds filter accordingly.

User Story: As the system, I want to query attendance by session so that trainers see who attended
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): getBySession() WHERE session_id, optional status. getSessionAttendance() formats.

User Story: As the system, I want hours-attended per user per edition so that certificates show time invested
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): getHoursAttended(): countsAsAttended records→sumDurationHours() one query.

User Story: As the system, I want attendance rate per user per edition so that reports show participation%
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): getAttendanceRate(): (attended/total)*100. 0 if no sessions.

User Story: As a trainer, I want to bulk-mark users present so that I don't mark each individually
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): markMultiplePresent(): loops markPresent(). Returns userId=>result. Non-atomic.

User Story: As the system, I want attendance to trigger completion checks so that enrollment transitions to Completed
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): EditionCompletion listener on stride/attendance/marked evaluates hours/rate.

User Story: As a student, I want to view my personal info so that I can verify what's stored
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): first/last from wp_users; phone/organisation/department from user_meta. Email read-only 'Neem contact op om je e-mailadres te wijzigen.' Display mode; edit via 'Bewerken'.

User Story: As a student, I want to edit my personal info so that my profile stays current
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): Alpine x-model; POST stride_update_profile form_type=personal. Partial update; empty wipes field. display_name recomputed. Toast 'Persoonlijke gegevens bijgewerkt.'

User Story: As a student, I want to update billing info so that quotes are issued correctly
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): billing_company/vat/address_1/postcode/city/invoice_email/gln_number. Partial update. Empty invoice_email falls back to user email. 'Facturatiegegevens bijgewerkt.'

User Story: As a student, I want to select my profile type so that I'm categorised correctly
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): Only renders if getTypes() non-empty. Dropdown with colour badges. Empty → 'Niet ingesteld'. 'Profieltype bijgewerkt.'

User Story: As a student, I want to manage notification prefs so that I only get wanted emails
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): Checkboxes reminders/new_courses/newsletter (yes/no). Language nl/fr/en default nl (allowlist). Partial update. 'Meldingsvoorkeuren bijgewerkt.'

User Story: As a student, I want to export my data so that I exercise GDPR right of access
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): stride_gdpr_export → wp_create_user_request export_personal_data. Confirmation email. 'Je ontvangt een bevestigingsmail om de export te starten.'

User Story: As a student, I want to request erasure so that I exercise right to be forgotten
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): Two-stage confirm. stride_gdpr_erase does NOT auto-delete: ndmail_send stride-gdpr-erasure-admin + do_action stride/gdpr/erasure_requested. 'De beheerder neemt zo snel mogelijk contact met je op.'

User Story: As a student, I want to log out so that I can end my session
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): Logout link href=wp_logout_url(home_url('/')). No API call.

User Story: As a student, I want to view all quotes (active+cancelled) so that I track costs
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): getQuoteData() returns active[]+cancelled[]. Active grouped+expanded; cancelled collapsible. No pagination.

User Story: As a student, I want to see quote details so that I know charges
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): Status badge Draft 'In behandeling'/Sent 'Verzonden'/Exported 'Verwerkt'/Cancelled 'Geannuleerd'. Subtotal/discount(+voucher)/21% tax/total. valid_until grey. Cancelled faded+strikethrough.

User Story: As a student, I want to download a quote PDF so that I file/send it
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): stride_quote_pdf?quote_id&nonce, new tab. Renders company/invoice-to/lines/subtotal/discount(+voucher)/tax/total. Fallback if data missing.

User Story: As a student, I want to edit billing on a draft quote so that the invoice is correct
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): Inline-edit only if Draft/Sent. company/email/address/postcode/city/vat. stride_update_quote. 'Offerte bijgewerkt.' Redirect after save.

User Story: As a student, I want an error on a locked quote so that I know it's finalised
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): validateQuoteAccess(): quote['locked']→WP_Error('locked','Deze offerte is vergrendeld door de beheerder en kan niet meer worden bijgewerkt.'). Blocks billing edit + voucher.

User Story: As a student, I want to apply a voucher to a draft quote so that I get a discount
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): Only if Draft/Sent AND no voucher applied. stride_apply_quote_voucher. Success 'Kortingscode toegepast!' then reload. Error in red.

User Story: As a student, I want clear errors for bad vouchers so that I know why
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): not found→'Voucher niet gevonden'; inactive→'Voucher is niet meer geldig'; used>=limit→'Voucher is uitgeput'; expired→'Voucher is verlopen'; not-yet→'Voucher is nog niet geldig'; scope→scoped error.

User Story: As a student, I want to cancel a quote before export so that I'm not charged if I change my mind
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): 'Annuleren' if status!=Exported. Two-stage confirm. Sets Cancelled. releaseVoucher() reverses redemption. Redirect ?tab=offertes.

User Story: As a student, I want to see quote validity so that I know when to act
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): 'Geldig tot <date>' grey if valid_until set. stride_format_date().

User Story: As a student in a klassikaal edition with optional sessions, I want session price modifiers in my quote so that I know cost impact
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): items[] base 'edition' + 'session_modifier' per selected optional session (can be negative). Recalc on onSessionSelectionCompleted. Non-draft blocks modifier changes (fires session_modifier_blocked).

User Story: As a student with a voucher, I want to see discount + code so that I know the deduction
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): discount row only if !isZero(). Voucher code grey '(CODE)'. '-€ X,XX' green. Discount capped at subtotal.

User Story: As a student, I want only my own quotes so that billing is private
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): getQuoteData() queries current user only. validateQuoteOwnership(): quote['user_id']===userId.

User Story: As an admin, I want vouchers to track usage so that I know when exhausted
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): used_count incremented via transaction (FOR UPDATE). used>=limit→status exhausted. Release decrements; reverts to Active if below limit.

User Story: As an admin, I want to scope vouchers to editions so that they're only redeemable there
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): scope_mode ''/'only'(edition_id)/'except'(excluded_edition_ids). ScopeValidator vs quote edition_id. Fail→scoped WP_Error.

User Story: As an admin, I want validity dates so that vouchers redeem only in a window
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): valid_from/valid_until vs current_time. now<from→'nog niet geldig'; now>until→'verlopen'. Empty=always valid.

User Story: As an admin, I want percentage/fixed/full vouchers so that I offer different incentives
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): DiscountType Full/Fixed/Percentage. Percentage capped 100%; Fixed capped at subtotal. apply_mode single_session prorates.

User Story: As an admin, I want to prevent double-redemption by one user so that the limit isn't abused
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): redemptions[] {user_id,quote_id,redeemed_at}. If user_id found→WP_Error('already_redeemed','Je hebt deze voucher al gebruikt'). Atomic transaction.

User Story: As a student, I want 21% VAT calculated correctly so that I know the tax
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): tax=round((subtotal-discount)*0.21) via QuoteCalculator. 'BTW (21%)'. Money cents precision.

User Story: As an admin, I want to anonymise a user keeping registrations so that I comply with retention
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): anonymise(): user_login/email/display_name/first/last/description stripped. Deletes mapped meta + EXTRA_PII. New random password. _stride_anonymised_at set. Idempotent. Refuses admins/stride_manage. Demotes to subscriber.

User Story: As an admin, I want to see when a user was anonymised so that I audit lifecycle
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): If anonymised, user-edit shows 'Geanonimiseerd op <date>' from _stride_anonymised_at.

User Story: As an admin, I want an 'Anonimiseer' row action instead of hard-delete so that I don't orphan registrations
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): Filters user_row_actions: replaces 'Verwijderen' with admin_post stride_anonymise_user. Hard delete still available. Admin notice on redirect.

User Story: As a student, I want consistent dashboard tabs so that nav doesn't disappear between sections
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): Single nav data structure. Sidebar(lg+) + mobile dock same primary (home/inschrijvingen/trajecten/offertes)+utility (meldingen/downloads/certificaten/profiel). Active aria-current. ?tab=slug. Rail state in localStorage.

User Story: As an admin, I want to CRUD editions so that I manage offerings
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): vad_edition CPT. Metabox: title/course(Select2)/dates/capacity/price(cents)/venue. edit_post + nonce stride_edition_nonce. Status dropdown (9 OfferingStatus). Sortable list, filter by course/status/format. Bulk delete disabled.

User Story: As an admin, I want to manage sessions so that meeting days are scheduled
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): vad_session CPT. AJAX stride_add/update/delete_session, nonce+edit_post. type validated vs SessionType. webinar_link esc_url_raw. price_modifier float→cents. wp_delete_post(true) on delete.

User Story: As an admin, I want notes on an edition so that I track todo/email/info
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): JSON array meta 'notes' {type,content,author,date}. Modal add; soft-delete by _deleted. type-specific icon/badge. sanitize_textarea_field. Reset on duplicate.

User Story: As an admin, I want a participants modal so that I manage registrations
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): Query registrations for edition, batch users+attendance. Name/email from user or enrollment_data (anonymous). Status, enrolled_by, registered_at. Per-session attendance grid. Approve/Reject/Approve-Post buttons via AJAX.

User Story: As an admin, I want to duplicate an edition so that I reuse a setup
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): duplicate(): new post + merged fields + FIELD_RESETS (notes/documents/selection_open). ' (kopie)'. Sessions copied date=today. Terms copied. status=draft. Row action edit_post+nonce.

User Story: As an admin, I want to mark attendance per session + bulk so that participation is tracked
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): AJAX stride_mark_attendance (session/user/status). unmarked deletes. stride_bulk_attendance marks all confirmed present. Returns presentCount/totalCount. Fires stride/attendance/marked.

User Story: As an admin, I want to lock/unlock all quotes for an edition so that prices stop changing
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): bulkSetLockedByEdition() AJAX stride_bulk_lock_quotes. Returns total/changed/unchanged. Locked blocks session modifiers. Toast 'N offertes vergrendeld'.

User Story: As an admin, I want to export registrations as multi-sheet XLSX so that I report/process
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): stride_export_registrations: Overzicht/Deelnemers/Facturatie/Aanwezigheid/Taken sheets. Batch users+quotes+attendance. CSV-injection safe (strings). export-{title}-{date}.xlsx.

User Story: As an admin, I want to export name cards so that I print badges
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): EditionNamecardExporter formats printer-friendly labels. type=namecards.

User Story: As an admin, I want to export attendance CSV so that I archive it
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): EditionAttendanceExporter: sessions×participants grid with status. type=attendance.

User Story: As a student, I want to log in via magic link or password so that I access my account
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): requestMagicLink() token+ndmail, rate 3/email,10/IP per 15min. Password login 5/IP/15min, wp_check_password. Both check consent.isActivated. Sets auth cookie.

User Story: As an anonymous user, I want to register so that I get an account
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): register() POST /registreren, rate 3/IP/15min. Creates WP_User, activation email token (one-time, 7-day). Activation sets consent+logs in. Data encrypted in transient.

User Story: As a student, I want to reset my password via magic link so that I regain access
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): requestMagicLink() (no password needed). Verified at auth/verify/{token}, sets cookie, user updates password. Single-use 7-day.

User Story: As an anonymous user, I want rate-limit errors so that abuse is prevented
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): isRateLimited('magic_email_'.email) transient count. 3/email, 5/IP password, 3/IP registration. 'Wacht even voordat je een nieuw inloglink aanvraagt'. Counter on every attempt.

User Story: As an admin, I want wp-login redirected to /aanmelden so that branding is consistent
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): Config redirect_wp_login=true → login_init redirects wp-login.php to login_url '/aanmelden'.

User Story: As an admin, I want to impersonate a student so that I debug their experience
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): POST /admin/users/{id}/impersonate. validateTarget rejects !manage_options, id<=0, target-is-admin. 64-hex token transient {admin_id,target_id} 3600s. Cookie stride_impersonate_token. wp_set_auth_cookie(target). End restores admin.

User Story: As an admin, I want impersonation audited so that actions are traceable
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): Rejects admin-to-admin. Starts logged via do_action. End requires valid transient (token+admin_id match cookie). AuditBridge logs events.

User Story: As an admin, I want an audit log of all actions so that I track lifecycle
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): AuditBridge listens stride/* + completion/* → AuditService.record(entity_type,id,action,actor_id,context). Events: registration/attendance/session/quote/trajectory/completion/user/mail/assistant.

User Story: As an admin, I want to reveal a sensitive field with logging so that access is accountable
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): GET /admin/users/{id}/reveal?field. stride_manage. Decrypted value + audit do_action ntdst/reveal_sensitive_field (field,user_id,admin_id). Field names whitelisted.

User Story: As a partner, I want to list my company users so that I manage them
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): GET /partner/users. partner role + company_id>0. WP_User_Query meta _stride_company_id. Pagination per_page max 100. {id,email,first_name,last_name,registered_at}.

User Story: As a partner, I want to list company enrollments so that I track them
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): GET /partner/enrollments. Filters status/edition_id/user_id. user_id verified vs company. findByCompany(). Trajectory parents nest child_registrations. {id,user_id,edition,course,trajectory,status,...}.

User Story: As a partner, I want a single enrollment's details so that I review it
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): GET /partner/enrollments/{id}. Verifies company_id (403 if not). Full data + nested children for parents.
Errors Found: PartnerAPICascadeTest direct-edition enrollment: NTDST_Query_Cache::onPostSave() fatal — strictly-typed WP_Post param, null given on save_post.
P3 Fix Status: FIXED: onPostSave param nullable + get_post() fallback + WP_Post guard (QueryCache.php)
P4 Re-test: PASS — PartnerAPICascadeTest green after onPostSave null-guard

User Story: As a partner, I want to list company certificates so that I track completions
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): GET /partner/certificates. learndash_user_activity course completed>0, user IN company. Pagination. {user,course,completed_at,certificate_url}.

User Story: As a partner, I want attendance records so that I monitor participation
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): GET /partner/attendance. Filters edition/user (max 50). user_id verified vs company. getByUsers(). Session hours from start/end. {user,session,status,hours}.

User Story: As a partner, I want to enroll a user via API so that I onboard staff
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): POST /partner/enrollments. user_email + edition_id|trajectory_id + create_user. Creates+affiliates if create_user. Verifies company (422/403). EnrollmentService::enroll/TrajectorySelection::enroll PATH_PARTNER. 201/400/404/409/422.
P4 Re-test: PASS — integration green after onPostSave null-guard

User Story: As a partner, I want company scoping enforced so that I only see my company
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): checkPermission() company_id. List endpoints filter by company_id. user_id param verified. findByCompany() WHERE company_id. 403 'User does not belong to your company'.

User Story: As an admin, I want annual KPIs so that I report yearly
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): AnnualReportPage submenu (stride_view). buildReport(year): enrollments/unique_participants/unique_organisations/completions/completion_rate/training_hours/editions_ran/sessions_ran. Current vs previous year.

User Story: As an admin, I want a PDF of the annual report so that I distribute it
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): stride_annual_report_pdf, nonce, year clamped 2000-2100. DOMPDF isRemoteEnabled=false, DejaVu Sans. Tables only. jaarrapport-{year}.pdf.

User Story: As an admin, I want CSV of the annual report so that I analyse data
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): stride_annual_report_csv, nonce. Formula-injection safe. Per-section or combined.

User Story: As an admin, I want to enable/disable client brand mu-plugins so that I swap branding
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): stride-client-* enabled via .off rename / config. Override templates/assets/colors. apply_filters('stride/brand'). One active at a time.
P2 Test Result: PASS (manual): VAD launch brand loads (tracked .php); other 4 brands off via .php.off. Swap = rename .php<->.php.off; verified activate+deactivate.

User Story: As a student, I want brand-specific templates/colors/logo so that the instance is branded
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): Active client hooks wp_enqueue_scripts adds CSS/logo. Templates override via addPath(). Logo/colors/typography from brand config.
P2 Test Result: PASS (manual): VAD active -> frontend enqueues stride-client-vad/assets/client.css (branded CSS applied).

User Story: As a student, I want email confirmation on interest/waitlist so that I know I'm registered
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): onInterestRegisteredUserMail/onWaitlistRegisteredUserMail on events. ndmail_send template. Name/email from WP_User or enrollment_data (anonymous). SmartCodes evaluated.

User Story: As an admin, I want email on new enrollment so that I'm aware
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): scheduleAdminNotify() on registration/created → wp_schedule_single_event(1s). Async send to getAdminEmail() (stride_admin_email option).

User Story: As a student, I want task completion emails so that I'm guided
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): onTaskCompleted() on enrollment/task_completed. Task-specific template. completion.url → /edities/{slug}/voltooien/.

User Story: As an admin, I want to email a quote with audit so that sending is traceable
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): onQuoteSendEmail(quote_id,to,cc) → ndmail_send stride-quote-email. AuditBridge logs quote.email_sent {to,cc}.

User Story: As a student, I want in-app notifications for admin actions so that I stay informed
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): getNotifications(): AuditService.getForSubjectUser + session-note updates. Maps to {id,type,title,body,url,timestamp,read}. Unread badge cached 10s. markAllRead/markRead → _stride_notifications_read.

User Story: As a student, I want operational logs excluded from notifications so that the feed is clean
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): EXCLUDED_ACTIONS=['mail.sent'] never surface even with context.user_id.

User Story: As an admin, I want SmartCode templates + dynamic triggers so that mails are personalised
Expected Behaviour (code-derived): registerSmartCodes() groups edition/registration/completion/quote/certificate/trajectory/gdpr with resolvers. registerTriggers() maps event→template. maybeSeedTemplates on admin_init.
